title: NASA's Sonic Boom Killer Just Broke the Sound Barrier for the First Time
script: NASA's experimental X-59 jet crossed Mach 1 for the first time on June 5, 2026. The plane hit Mach 1.1 at 43,000 feet above the California desert. The X-59 is NASA's answer to the Concorde problem: it flies supersonic but generates a quiet thump instead of a boom. If regulators approve, overland supersonic flights could return for the first time in 50 years. Concorde retired in 2003 because the sonic boom was too loud. That excuse just ran out.
